title: Contact Form 7 Integration

posts.author: steveduffy (Steve Duffy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Morning all

I’ve had a look to see if this has been mentioned elsewhere and couldn’t find it so apologies if repeating a post but I wanted to know if there was an easy way to integrate my sites web forms to Monday.com.

Currently use and Avada site which comes with Avada Forms but also have the Contact Form 7 plugin as well so can use this if necessary.

Everywhere I’m looking just sends me to yet more tech app companies that are charging another $30 a month!

Regards

Steve

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @steveduffy - Your least expensive option right now is to see if you can send the data from Contact Form 7 on WordPress to either Zapier or Integromat, and then send it into monday․com that way. There is no public app for Contact Form 7 that I’m aware of.

~ Deb Cinkus
Product-certified Partner

Want to chat live with other monday․com fans & users?
Join us on Discord: The Monday Neighborhood

posts.author: Sophiaapin (Sophia Pin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @steveduffy ,

I found this plugin that copies Contact From 7 submissions to Monday.com:

[https://flagstaffintegrations.com/integration-for-contact-form-7-and-monday-com/]

It isn’t free, but it’s quite a bit cheaper than Zappier or Integromat. It’s also a lot easier to use.

Hope this helps!

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @steveduffy

I just wanted to jump in here and let you know that we do have a native form feature within monday.com.
This would avoid the need to integrate with another form provider.
The forms do have an embed code, so could be integrate into your website
You can read more about our forms feature here: The Forms

If you’d like to stick with your current provider then the other suggestions above are both great options to achieve this!

I hope this helps! If one of the answers in this thread has resolved your question, please don’t forget to mark it as the solution!

Best,

Dani

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @steveduffy

It looks like you’ve got some great recommendations from @Dani and @Sophiaapin!

We want to make sure that anyone else who happens on this thread can find the answer quickly and easily, so make sure to mark the comment that answered your questions as the solution!

If you’ve got any new questions, feel free to make a new post

Cheers,
Jenna

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @steveduffy

To help close this out, I’ve made a summary of the proposed solutions here since we don’t have an internal integration with Contact Form 7:

  1. Use our internal Form capabilities: The Forms

  2. Use one of our no-code 3rd party platform integrations to build your own integration with Contact Form 7:

  • How to create an integration using Zapier
  • How to create an automation or integration with Integromat
  1. This plugin @Sophiaapin found that copies Contact From 7 submissions to Monday.com:
  • https://flagstaffintegrations.com/integration-for-contact-form-7-and-monday-com/

If you still need help, feel free to open a new thread and we can go from there

Cheers,
Jenna
